filmEnPlot: Short animated film
Short animated film

Two strange birds get to know each other over dinner. The owl, a perfect hostess, serves one course after the other. The oystercatcher is in for a long evening filled with awkward moments and unbearable silence.\n\n
Short animated film
At the heart of an animation studio, three young orangutans join forces to recreate a jungle born of imagination.\n\n

filmEnTitle: Žarko, You Will Spoil the Child!
filmEnPlot: Recounting childhood memories in Split, Croatia in the 1980s.
Short animated film
Entirely animated and composited in Blender with additional 3D assets from Blenderkit and additional sound effects from Freesound.org\n
A girl left for dead gets devoured by forest spirits.\n
An ancestral house builds itself, comes to life, and shows us its story spanning one hundred fifty years. Through the ages, it allows us to perceive the passage of time.\n

A scene inspired by the Seto Inland Sea is depicted in the style of a sumi-e painting, set to a violin version of the koto piece “HARU NO UMI (The Spring Sea)” by Japanese koto player and composer Miyagi Michio in 1929.\n\n

Legends have been created for centuries. They talk about the structure of the world, the origin of rivers and mountains. The film is based on one of the legends about the beautiful Katun and her lover Biya. The film's characters are our young contemporaries who travel across Altai.
Join us aboard the luggage rack of my bicycle to enjoy a sensory roller coaster as it passes through a territory full of tricks.\n
In the midst of war, a man watches his beloved one by lighting the three matches he has left. Then, in the pitch darkness, a forest grows from their lovemaking and protects them from the war.\n\n
Some places in the world are better for business than others. And with the right skills, you don’t just meet demand — you create it.\n

Anemoia is an animated short film with a dreamlike tone that dissolves the fragile border between memory, dream and reality. While resting in the silent ruins of an ancient civilization, the protagonist slips into a light sleep. At the heart of the film is a deep nostalgia for a past never lived, a poignant desire for an unattainable time that exists only in the soul.
A gridded series of over 1500 improvised drawings in a tiny sketchbook, each smaller than a centimeter, compiled digitally into an animation.\n

A doctor was treating a boy with a strange illness; the boy was constantly tumbling over himself, causing him great pain. Later, the doctor discovered that this strange illness was actually related to his family relationships.\n

Nora and Thao, two friends finishing their studies, drift through surreal landscapes in a timeless journey. As they go, they laugh, have fun, discuss the future, and are surprised by feelings they did not suspect.\n\n
While discussing their deepest fears on a train ride, two friends are interrupted by an unusual passenger.\n
Bruce, a young theatre director, is desperately trying to rehearse the perfect birthday only to realize that the unexpected cannot be avoided in his play nor in his life.\n\n

A film that uses driving practice to explore the warmth and conflicts of everyday life. Five distinct characters navigate lessons marked by mishaps from pig transport accidents to roadside breakdowns, culminating in a quiet bond between the coach and his son.\n

A perfect day at the beach is suddenly disturbed, a rowdy crowd of Oldies shuffle down the dunes, disregarding the norms and living as they please. Sun baking, kissing and getting on the piss, It’s the oldies of the coast.\n
Mom is the name of a little girl who spends her childhood in Cambodia
Cave drawings come to life, chasing for the prey.\n\n
Living for her acrobatic passion, legless Carla rebels against contemporary social norms.\n

In Persian, the word “tar” carries a dual meaning: both a strand of hair and a traditional Iranian string instrument. In this – quite literally – hairy animation, an angry centipede storms a young girl’s inner world, shattering musical instruments and their delicate sounds.\n
Within microscopic life, I see loneliness overlapping with a longing for something. There, a gentle waver seems to carry a hope to belong somewhere, or perhaps a wish to remain pure.\n
The wise bird wife sends three birds to guide the girl through the unknown dark landscapes of her inner world.
During a visit to a zoo, our gaze lingers on the last silhouettes and stories that still inhabit the place.

A cartoon about a first relationship and the mixed feelings associated with it. The young girl dreamed of romance, but her expectations did not match reality. Suddenly, a large humanoid hornet appears in her life and falls in love with her.
Animation means being full of life, so why would you make an animated film without animation?\n

Meeting the new millennium was a huge event for the youth of the 2000s. Or was it just an ordinary New Year’s Eve, without any UFO attack on planet Earth? The parents’ story becomes more interesting because their children are now 20 years old — exactly the same age their parents were when they celebrated the new millennium.\n
Knitting with fine threads is woven into her daughter's life at every stage of growing up. A story about maternal care and family unity through time.

filmEnTitle: A corner dear to my heart
filmEnPlot: Sometimes, under the pressure of circumstances, you have to leave even the most native lands. As time passes, the image of the place becomes more and more blurred, but it still occupies a separate space in the memory and heart.

One day I visited my parents, who live far from me, with a Polaroid camera. By the time I realized I had not captured my most beloved people, there were only three frames left in the cartridge. I was obsessed with trying to photograph my parents. But I could not do it. The importance of our moments seemed overwhelming, so the responsibility weighed me down. But in the end, it turns out I remember these unphotographed moments better than the captured ones.
A man comes to a private brothel in an apartment building, but gets the wrong floor.
The life and daily life of young girls after arrest, in a special detention center and before it.\n\n
"The heroes of my childhood were cops: I saw some on TV, others at my dad's job."

The film tells the story of the Nazino tragedy—the events of 1933 in the northern Tomsk region, when approximately 6,000 people were exiled to an uninhabited island, most of whom died within days. This event was the result of the inhumane policy of "cleansing large cities of so-called declassed elements," with people deported without charge. Numerous cases of cannibalism among prisoners and abuse of power by government officials are documented.\n

A film dedicated to the balcony in an apartment in my native Minsk, from which I am moving out. The sketch is an attempt to capture this place as I see it and remember it, so that I can safely say goodbye.

A group of different people, brought together at a summer school, are asked the same simple, everyday question: “What should be done?”

At first, their answers sound familiar — errands, plans, ordinary things to take care of. But sometimes, in a pause, a glance, or a moment of silence, something deeper unexpectedly emerges: a philosophical reflection.

In this work, we sought to capture our vision of what passes through people’s minds at the very moment they respond to our question. The animation preserves these seconds of honesty, turning a philosophical question into a documentary discovery.
In a huge underwater city, a very small fish sails. Is he too small to see the world like the big ones?

The World of Birds meets to choose its sovereign. The golden eagle takes the throne. He saw no other member of their community worthy of the title. Indeed, no one dares oppose the proud and powerful animal. Except... the kinglet. In his small voice, he boldly offered to do the same. The eagle accepts his wager: the one who flies highest in the sky will wear the crown.\n

In an infinite night, the Great Buffalo Goddess meets the Little Goat Goddess. A friendship is born, and with it a new world: stars, sun, earth and living creatures. Animals emerge from the waters, and a lush forest grows. As the world evolves, the two goddesses are overwhelmed by their power to create.\n

A little boy, bored with building a bed, seeks something more exciting. A mischievous fire creature lures him away, and despite his father’s attempts to keep order, the boy follows his new friend into a magical mushroom world where anything is possible.\n
A few exercises to procrastinate better, faster and more efficiently.\n\n

When the hands of the cuckoo clock strike, a wooden doll awakens to yet another cycle. Worlds shift, crumble, and rebuild as she searches desperately for a way out, yet time always rewinds before freedom can be reached.\n
Short Film made with Steve Roberts for the DePict competition at Bristol’s Encounters festival. Having to tell a story in 90 seconds.\n\n
An anxious young boy finds himself wandering through a twisted, fantastical world on his first day of school.\n\n
A dystopian and darkly whimsical animated short, unfolding in fragmented vignettes where characters collide – each caught in their own rhythm of time.\n

An animated suite in five movements that transforms urban decay and personal mourning into a visual refuge. Through the singularity of the small, and exploring the urban skin through the ephemeral traces of Valencia's street art, Microcosmos is an experimental documentary animation that immerses us in the collection of existence.\n\n

A routine work trip for married couple Hana and Kornel suddenly turns into an adventurous struggle for survival. In a remote forest, far from civilization, they are forced to face not only the wilderness, but also the fragile terrain of their long-term, stagnant relationship.
When immersed in the contours swelling and waning in the forest of needles, the mundane world drifts away, and we only live to breathe.\n\n
Through the opening between reality and subtle poetic forms, surreal scenes and thrilling paradoxes reveal endless loops of existence.\n

filmEnTitle: How To Walk, a guide for fun and profit
filmEnPlot: This film emerged from the rhythm and textures of a piece of music by Vern Avola called Trigger Object. It’s a study of locomotion, growth and societal collapse in the form of an animated walk cycle.\n\n

filmEnTitle: yellow orange green blue red cut
filmEnPlot: yellow bellow can you get me to orange so i can see green but that won't do because i still need to smell blue and that can't be too far from red.\n
Short animated film

An anxious monster spots a bug and thinks it has crawled out of his brain. A simple chase turns into a spiraling adventure through shifting landscapes, where nothing stays still — not even his sense of self.\n\n
Anton observes. Alone. A phosphorescent glow flickers through the streets. Anton sees a bee dying, then another. No one sees a thing, not even Kata, over there on the roof...\n
The cartoon is a discussion about the fractality of destinies, the lightness of being and homesickness. The journey of a heroine who goes hunting and becomes the target of a predator herself.

filmEnTitle: As if the earth had swallowed them
filmOrigTitle: Como si la tierra se las hubiera tragado
filmEnPlot: Olivia, a young woman living abroad, returns to her hometown in Mexico in the hope of reconnecting with her past.
A simple itch descends into an outbreak with many legs. Certain things will always find their way to the surface.\n
The boy takes the knife his father once gave him. Did what followed make him a “man”?\n
After seeing a man lying in the snow, a boy recalls a St. Nicholas night spent with his father years ago. The boy expected his father to give him a toy as a gift, but it didn't go as expected.\n
Shavkat, a pizza courier, and his girlfriend Fatima are illegal migrants in a new land. When they crash their motorcycle while on delivery, Shavkat fears deportation and flees the scene.\n

Agathe, aged 90, lives as a recluse in a snow-covered cabin. Frede, her daughter, visits her with the intention of bringing her closer to her in a nursing home in town. But between the disembowelled fox on the doorstep and her mother's disturbing behaviour, the visit takes a different turn.\n
Five little girls are monkeying around. They play games, tell secrets, brush hair, run around, dress up, and jump, and giggle, and jump and jump and jump and jump and…\n
A lonely young man behind a smiling mask confronts his memories and his inner collapse, realising that freedom only comes when he lets himself fall and faces his true self.\n\n

The animated short "jam" is a deeply personal work born from the director’s experience of caring for a father who suffered a brain hemorrhage and the mother who steadfastly supported him. The title "jam" encapsulates the conflicting emotions of family life: the "congestion" of a life with no escape, the "viscous" weight of anxiety, and the "sweet salvation" found in a mother’s strength.\n\n

While taking a bath, a girl discovers flowers growing from her body. As she tries to explore these changes, she's drawn to a glowing orb peeking through the door. Following the orb, she witnesses her parents transformed into tigers in the bedroom, classmates turned into frogs on lotus leaves, and a runaway hotdog... Thus begins her fantastical journey through the mysteries of the body and growing up.\n

A snail is crushed by a car, a dog defecates while a tourist jumps from the world's tallest skyscraper. A series of fragments of everyday life spirals into chaos, culminating in the destruction of daily monotony.
Her disappearance began in the early hours of one morning. We took the wrong suitcase at the airport before the typhoon hit. After that, her life and ours have continued like this.\n\n
Candles are flickering. In the dark, endless dreamscape, where do I find myself?\n

There lives a little carefree girl, Maria, about whom there is a great providence in the history of mankind. Gradually, as she grows up, the heroine puts words in her heart that reveal the meaning of her future.\n
An animated video for the song "Heterochromia" by the Yakut band Neriberry. A light and dreamy story about bizarre lovers who met in the world of dreams.

Animated music video for the song "Spring.avi" from the band Cosmos on the Ceiling in collaboration with Ivan Serdyuk. An abstract love story about two flowers who can't figure out their place in each other's lives.
Every person is a separate world filled with experiences, emotions, and feelings that can sometimes consume them. But what happens when someone wants to immerse themselves in that world?\n

The video for Miguel Aceves Meia's song "El Pastor".\nWatercolor landscapes of Mexico replace each other. The air, the mountains, the rocks, the cacti, the music. A day in the life of a shepherd. Is a flock of sheep floating on the mountains or clouds?
The boy is riding with friends on the carousel and suddenly notices that one of the carousel horses is broken.

This story happened 25-60 thousand years ago with a great teacher, during a period of unprecedented storms. or this story happened yesterday on a soft and cozy armchair under the supervision of a therapist. or this story never happened...
The video for Sveta Ben's song "Don't be Sad" is a collective student work. "Don't be sad about anything \ Everything around you is not forever \ Everything that moves will find \ A wooden box..."
Short animated film
A Mourner comes to a house filled with sorrow to feed on the power of someone else's grief. But her crying breaks the barrier between the worlds, and dark creatures enter the house.

“Sea, take it away” is a stop-motion music video for a song by the musician menya zovut masha.\nIt reflects on exile and belonging, the pull of the sea, and the process of growing up and learning to accept change.
Animated music video
experimental animation about sensorial memory. What rests after the journey exept facts\n
It hurts! Everything hurts, from the bottom to the top, every part of her body! It drives her insane! But the doctors keep saying - she's in perfect health. So, what's the reason behind that pain?
A fire ignites, and a boy's fantasies about a girl dissolve into a haunting reality he can't escape.\n
One day, the granddaughter notices that instead of her grandmother, a big child now lives in the room.

When Nina and her best friend Lily decide to steal an eyeliner, they hope that this will be their key to womanhood. When they’re given wildly different receptions to wearing it, Nina discovers that her journey as a trans girl might not be so easy.\n
After her grandfather’s absence, a young girl wonders about the future of the family farm.\n\n

The plot of the film is a modern reinterpretation of P.P. Bazhov's fairy tale "Cherry Pebbles". The girl, treating everyone and everything around her without respect, is trying to get beautiful green pebbles. This behavior leads the heroine to a sad result.

filmEnTitle: Cultivation, Harvesting and Dispersal of Thorns
filmEnPlot: The cultivation of thorns requires several stages. Planting, rain, patience and harvest.
Setting out to escape insomnia, hunger, and self-doubt. Three seals embarked on a journey about themselves. From ocean to desert, the three seals find nothing. Until they discover Turning Hill.….\n\n
Life in the forest hides a lot of dangers and not only little birds hard to live there, but also great hunters.\n
Eager to create a monumental work of art, a shaman remains blissfully unaware that she is painting her murals in the mouth of a vain weather presenter.

In a city—evoking, without specificity, a city of the Roman Empire—the poem depicts a state of waiting: the arrival of the “Barbarians” has been announced, the emperor himself seems ready to submit to their authority, and every hypothesis about the future is possible. The city holds its breath in anticipation… But who, exactly, are these “Barbarians”? And what if they do not come?\n\n
The film is about a bunch of cars driving at night and the craziness that results.
Two in a room. Total control of the senses. The forest outside the window terrifies with its freedom and love of life…\n

In this experimental animated film, a woman explores the layers of her own body and inner world. Through a symbolic depiction of separation and reconnection with herself, the film examines the dynamics of roughness and tenderness in self-perception. An intimate journey that delves into the complexity of self-acceptance and inner harmony.\n\n
Two creatures meet on a tree. Their encounter goes deep down to the roots and beyond.\n

filmEnTitle: Biting the Hand that Feeds You
filmEnPlot: The contest is called "Payday," and Chantal is in a rush because she has a lot to say on the subject. For once, she ticks all the boxes: director, woman... She's ready to start taking notes, drawing... But all too soon, her everyday reality comes rushing back.\n

In seclusion at La Quinta del Sordo, Goya confronts illness, memories, and haunting visions, exorcising his demons through the Black Paintings—sacrificing peace, sanity, and self to immortalize his torment.\n
A mysterious story about a man who gets stuck in a small provincial hotel where strange things happen and the guests die one by one.
A day in the life of the boy who met a calf.\n

Ordinary life repeats itself every day. But the succession of moments that we repeat over and over again is never the same; everything changes and wavers. The moment we touch something, our consciousness can go to a place that does not exist anywhere in the past, nor in the future. In an uncertain and fluctuating world, only the feeling of the body touching something, which exists only in this present reality, can be recognised as “now”. That moment is lovely.\n
A girl made a wish with her lost teeth. That night, there were actually two tooth fairies came to the little girl's room. Who is the real tooth fairy?\n

filmOrigTitle: Les funérailles de la princesse limace
filmEnPlot: The journey of a solitary pilgrim, transporting a mysterious young girl to her final resting place. It is the story of a funeral procession through the ruins of a lost civilization, vast cemetery of robots populated by luminous creatures who seem intrigued by their presence.\n\n

In a society living in box car apartments, encircled with darkness and polluted with neon lights and scaffolding, glowing eyes creatures take on a journey to free themselves from their personal demons. They must leave their dark habitats and venture into even darker corridors, tunnels and underground passages of their worlds in search of relief.\n

What is a body without a horse? In an attempt to identify itself, the horseless body moves through its absences and voids, measuring its outlines, folding into itself, tearing, dreaming, recomposing, just to find itself.\n

Dedicated to the memory of environmentalists imprisoned in Iran, this is a story about people who are like nature. A deer headed man is arrested, but his community remembers him for the kind soul he is — not for the monster he is painted as.\n
Family trip is interrupted in the middle of the day, when the fabric of reality gets thin enough to reveal what this summer day is really like.\n
A child is like a morning glory, blossoming within the tender yet binding embrace of maternal love.\n\n

filmEnTitle: Wandering Dot and the Blank Square Page Universe
filmOrigTitle: Klīstošais punktiņš un tukšās kvadrātveida lapas visums
filmEnPlot: Is a blank page ever really empty? Where does an artwork begin or end? Are you the one directing creative flow or is it leading you? One may feel a creative impulse, but confronting daunting emptiness as a point of departure is inevitable. The only thing left to do is to make the first step, first dot, line or movement and suddenly the page transforms into something completely else and the multitudes it contains take up life on its own.\n

There are only two roles in the world: victim and aggressor/ sheep and wolf. Each of them lives by clear, understandable rules: the sheep is afraid of the wolf, the wolf eats the sheep. But is it possible to change your role, or do you have to accept it?

Most people will encounter their second “weaning” in life. We often take for granted that the relationships between people are unbreakable. But when we lose connection with the outside world, how do we face ourselves? How do we find meaning in solitude?\n

filmOrigTitle: Emechiche, el renacer de los cabeciblancos
filmEnPlot: In the Montes de María region of Colombia lives the Emechiche, an endangered species. Observing their daily life reveals the complex relationships they have with their offspring, their environment and the strategies that sustain their survival.\n
70-year-old Timo makes the most of his short ride to work. Speeding up on a bicycle ends up in a ditch, but the adrenaline rush leaves a feeling of pleasure.\n
A new cartoon by Alyona Tomilova ("Cartoon ether").

filmEnTitle: A Shepherd Boy, a Ram and a Girl
filmEnPlot: The little girl wants to deliver to shepherd his lunch, but she can't find him anywhere.\n
It hurts! Everything hurts, from the bottom to the top, every part of her body! It drives her insane! But the doctors keep saying - she's in perfect health. So, what's the reason behind that pain?

filmEnTitle: My little life on the island
filmEnPlot: The boy is left in the care of his older teenage brother, who has no time to play with a small child, considering himself an adult. Naturally, they quarrel. The boy, upset with his brother, goes to live on an island. But here’s a surprise: the island turns out to be floating and takes the boy on an incredible journey into a world of animals, birds, and fish. The brother must bring the boy home, but he is terrified of water.\n
One day, the granddaughter notices that instead of her grandmother, a big child now lives in the room.

The girl and with her the viewer, go to a nostalgic "far away", where the past comes to life. This is a metaphor for generational change and knowledge transfer. The plot is based on the folk nursery rhyme "Forty-forty, where have you been? "Far away." The bird "steals" carelessness, and the heroine tries to return it, but is confronted with the conventions of the world - as in the rhyme: "you didn't carry water ...". The ending may be open: the toy may not always be returned, but the memories remain.\n

Little Max has a nightmare in which his hands are hoppled and in front of him Max sees a long ladder, leading to the beautiful space upstairs. But he cannot climb it without the help of his hands and he falls down into the dark cellar, where he sees a teacher and pupils with hoppled hands there. Max feels scared and wakes up. Luckily, it was just a dream… But is his life truly free and easy in the real world? And where do such dreams come from? Max is a deaf boy from a deaf family. And to develop his speech, his school insists on communication without the use of sign language. But when it is your native language – through it you think and feel, imagine and play – how can you make it stop talking?\n

The plot of the film is a modern reinterpretation of P.P. Bazhov's fairy tale "Cherry Pebbles". The girl, treating everyone and everything around her without respect, is trying to get beautiful green pebbles. This behavior leads the heroine to a sad result.
Life in the forest hides a lot of dangers and not only little birds hard to live there, but also great hunters.\n

The boy wakes up in the morning and gets ready for school. Seeing the city landscape outside the window, he climbs into a submarine and flies through the city in it. He swims to school and gets a deuce in class, escapes in a boat to the park.\n
A day in the life of the boy who met a calf.\n

Legends have been created for centuries. They talk about the structure of the world, the origin of rivers and mountains. The film is based on one of the legends about the beautiful Katun and her lover Biya. The film's characters are our young contemporaries who travel across Altai.
A New Year's story about an uninvited guest who managed to make someone else's holiday his own.\n

There are only two roles in the world: victim and aggressor/ sheep and wolf. Each of them lives by clear, understandable rules: the sheep is afraid of the wolf, the wolf eats the sheep. But is it possible to change your role, or do you have to accept it?
The cartoon is a discussion about the fractality of destinies, the lightness of being and homesickness. The journey of a heroine who goes hunting and becomes the target of a predator herself.
The film is a journey through the memories of the main character, who once chose solitude. A young man consciously withdraws from the world until the echoes of the past force him to face his fears.

In the forest, a hunter gets caught in a snowstorm. He hears a terrifying female
startled scream, and rushes to help. He finds a girl surrounded by 3 bears. The girl shows the
hunter that she was picking berries and rushes into his arms, only for him to save her. She kisses
him, passing the juice of the berries through the kiss. The hunter is about to pierce the bears
with an arrow, protecting the girl, but at the last moment before shooting, the girl pushes him
away. The hunter sees that in front of him is not a girl, but a witch, and the juice of the berries
has been poisoned. The fight continues. The hunter cannot cope with poison, bears and moving
into the spirit world, but dies. The bears take him to the forest, and the girl starts picking berries
again, preparing for a new hunt.
The Wanderer carries the last seed through the ruins of the world.\n
Two in a room. Total control of the senses. The forest outside the window terrifies with its freedom and love of life…\n
Family trip is interrupted in the middle of the day, when the fabric of reality gets thin enough to reveal what this summer day is really like.\n

A cartoon about a first relationship and the mixed feelings associated with it. The young girl dreamed of romance, but her expectations did not match reality. Suddenly, a large humanoid hornet appears in her life and falls in love with her.

He has a seed in his heart but lives on a tiny island where nothing can grow. Worse, a rowdy band of musicians has moved in nearby and are driving him crazy. If he wants things to change, he'll have to look elsewhere.\n
Animated music video for the Juna — Iyun band

Mara, a young teenager, attends a league match where she unexpectedly crosses paths with Vera, her childhood best friend. As the game unfolds, she’s flooded with memories of the bright bond they once shared, and of the day Vera left the club, leaving behind an ache Mara never fully understood. Now, standing across from her and with a single decisive play ahead, Mara must confront the echo of that old wound—and choose whether to return the hurt she once carried.\n\n
Short animated music video

Animals drink and dance to the rhythm of the beat. The party is scorching. The air is heavy. The club is humid. The atmosphere is clammy. The walls are oozing. The space fills with liquid. The wildlife is submerged. The frantically moving bodies of the dancers form various ornaments and choreographic creations, shifting the animation to an abstract plane.\n\n
Music video directed by Simon Medard for Camera-etc.

This song by a Uruguayan band thematising melancholy works with the motif of a mosaic which gradually disintegrates. It reveals dream worlds – beautiful at first sight – which overflow with so many motifs and information that we feel overwhelmed. An important and symbolic role in this music video is played by ceramic and the art of kintsugi, which teaches us to love our inevitable scars.\n\n
In this story, a powerful monkey, born from a magical stone, is trapped beneath a mountain by Buddha himself after becoming too powerful and causing chaos in heaven.\n\n
A story about letting go of one's former self. It revolves around two characters: the Present Self and the Past Self.\n
Short animated film
Short animated film

“The Turns” is an experimental animation film created in 2025 by Colorado artist and filmmaker John Dawson. This is a 100% hand made film by drawing and painting directly onto 16mm film stock. It comes to life with a pulse, fast rhythm and abstraction that moves.\n

The music video is a drunken journey made solely of paper, combining cut-outs from videos of the singer and musician performing, mixed with handcolored, printed video-frames of videos from train and bus windows in different cities. \n\n
Animated music video
Short animated film
On the island of Réunion, in the Indian Ocean, a music called Maloya resonates. Larzan Karné is both a visual transcription of this music and a celebration of its rhythms.\n\n
A group of scientists stranded on an alien planet struggle against a mysterious force that holds them in its grip.
Short animated music video

filmEnTitle: El Cuarteto de Nos "En el cuarto de Nico"
filmEnPlot: Videoclip Oficial

“Dr. Dr. Themefart, Frustrating Himself, Posed As Gay Old Nazi SS” is ananagrammatic exorcism of David Bowie’s album title “The Rise and Fall of Ziggy Stardust and the Spiders From Mars”. This film explores the aesthetic possibilities and joys of metamorphosis via Artificial Intelligence – right along the lines of the Sound of Money’s liquifying play with the musical language of Rock’n Roll, and in the vein of the master metamorphician of Pop himself.\n\n
Short animated film
Short animated film

A hallucinatory journey through an abstract cave, Echo Cave follows the shifting motion of stones as they guide the viewer through a surreal space. Blurring the boundaries between the physical and the digital, the film explores illusions of matter and memory through hypnotic rhythms and AI-generated textures.\n

filmEnTitle: Leda and the Lord of the Reedy River
filmEnPlot: "Leda and the Lord of the Reedy River" is an animated short film created by American director Eric Schafer. Inspired by the classical Greek mythology of Leda and the Swan (famously echoed in Donovan's 1968 folk song "Lord of the Reedy River"), the film has been featured in international animation festivals.

No Man’s Land is an experimental hand-painting style animation made with AI-generated images and videos referenced by group members’ hand paintings. With the music “Odd Piano” produced by Kazuto Okawa/LLLL, the film walks with the audience through a complete journey of struggling with the darkness and finding the peace back in the end.\n\n

This somewhat frantic Japanese-Korean coproduction is characterised by shaky drawing, pulsating images and jerky animation. It depicts a neurotic cook who comes to the kitchen, cooks, cooks and then cooks some more, over and over again without a break until his death! As we watch him, we begin to perceive how the sequence of images accelerates and the end is inevitable.\n\n
Short animated film

filmEnTitle: La Ride : Partie 2 - Duke Mobb feat. Chilly Gonzales, Juliette Armanet, JeanJass, Tedax Max
filmEnPlot: Short animated music video

In their house in the middle of the forest, a brother and sister play, grow bored, watch time go by. The days pass, each one alike, save for a few details: a crack in the wall, a shadow too big, a burnt photograph… Night falls and the children’s room sinks into darkness.\n\n

Based on a true story of an ostrich 
escaping a safari park 
and dying of exhaustion 
after running 20 kilometers. 
Various artworks were inspired by this story. 
Yuan Shui distilled these into animation, 
symbolizing the subjective projections of people 
onto the ostrich and playfully reflecting 
how an ostrich might perceive humans.

The cartoon based on the Nagaibak culture was created in the Nagaibak language (Nagaibaks are a small—numbered people living only in the Chelyabinsk Region, who have preserved their national identity). This is not a film adaptation of a folk tale, it is an author's cartoon that combines the main folk symbols and motifs.

This story is about the relationship between the inhabitants of a small Mari village and the forest deity Yuma. The boy Yong goes to the forest for cranberries, where many dangers await him. Who should he trust: a squirrel with a nut? The cute wolf cub? To my fellow villagers and grandmother? Or Yume?

The story is about a boy and his dad who live in a small house on the edge of the steppe. The boy is confined to a wheelchair and can't even take a spoonful of porridge. Dad is close to despair, but everything changes when one day the latest robot landing on Mars is shown on TV.… This parable film is dedicated to all parents who don't give up.
The film immerses viewers in a world of unrestrained childish imagination, where getting messy and bathing in the tub is a joyful adventure\n
A story about little insects who staged a big concert\n

Yakut children Nyukku, Micheal and Ayaal are trying to solve the mysterious fortune-teller's prediction on the night of Tankha. Friends will be tested by mystical incidents and learn about what the future holds for them.
This is the story of the relationship between a teenage girl and her mother, a strict swimming coach. In training, my daughter goes into conflict, but suddenly it evokes her childhood memories.

Yuri lives far to the north, in a village near the cosmodrome. He is passionate about fantasy and dreams of an incredible discovery that will make him famous. One day, in the forest, he finds a UFO…Yuri lives far to the north, in a village near the cosmodrome. He is passionate about fantasy and dreams of an incredible discovery that will make him famous. One day, in the forest, he finds a UFO…\n

filmEnTitle: One day the Sun will explode
filmEnPlot: After the Sun explodes, humanity is gone — the main character finds herself as the last human left alive. A new civilization has taken Earth's place. Scared and disoriented, she refuses to accept this bizarre reality, while strange, curious creatures follow her every step\n
The story of first love, rivalry and true friendship, which will survive all love and rivalry, is created by director Anna Aladzhikova. It's based on her own childhood memories.

filmEnTitle: In the Shade of the Trees or the Ten Montenegrin Commandments
filmOrigTitle: В тени деревьев, или Десять черногорских заповедей
filmEnPlot: The shade of trees is salvation – no one has ever died from resting. The film is based on the Ten Montenegrin Commandments.

Inspired by W. B. Yeats’ poem The Stolen Child, this animated short by Olga Baulina tells the story of Yolash, a lonely boy caught between his parents’ quarrels. One day he follows a mysterious bird-girl and enters a world of half-bird, half-human creatures, where he unexpectedly becomes part of their ancient ritual.

The film explores fragility of human life, the longing for connection, and the wonder hidden in the world around us.

With Igor Kovalyov (Milch, Before Love, Bird in a Window, Flying Nansen) as Creative Art Director, the film brings together a unique artistic vision and the signature touch of one of animation’s most acclaimed auteurs.

The film was shot at Lisa Skvortsova's intensive course "Energy Injection". With the pulsating rhythm of the wheels, the train rushes into the unknown. A lone passenger unexpectedly meets a mysterious cat with a suitcase and strange things begin to happen.
The life of a travelling performer is not a life for everybody, but for some it is the only way they can express themselves.

filmEnTitle: At Christmas, everyone is a bit wise...
filmEnPlot: A film adaptation of Joseph Brodsky's poem "December 24, 1971."\n
A discerning shopper came to the market in search of the perfect watermelon...\n
During a walk with a dog the loss of glasses turned into a whole adventure.\n

filmEnTitle: There's a lake beyond the forest
filmEnPlot: A workshop based on the 19th BFM trailer was held in the space of the Big Animation School. Together with Nina Bisyarina, the laboratory participants made sketches of trees, birds and animals and learned how nature and animation are connected. At the end of the laboratory, each participant made an animated sketch, which became part of the micro-almanac. The premiere took place at the Closing Ceremony of the XIX Big Cartoon Festival.
The Forest Spirit brings a human dress to the magical world. This entails irreversible consequences for both worlds – human and fairy-tale.\n
After a hard day's work, people usually say they're coming home. I'm going back to the dorm. So much time has passed, but the tongue has not turned to call it home.

Kitty meets Hedgehog at the walls of a psychiatric hospital: he is a patient and an artist, she came from outside. Their relationship is rapidly gaining momentum, and soon the patients, along with doctors and nurses, are helping the couple to have a wedding. Kitty and Hedgehog begin a family life, but the new reality turns out to be a serious challenge for them.

The animation film is inspired by the work of the Kostroma Russian artist and writer Efim Chestnyakov, and is created in his artistic style. It's a utopian fairy tale about the transformation of the village of Shablovo into a city of the future, which begins with the construction of a huge tarantass—a collaborative effort from the entire community.\n

A granddaughter walks around her grandmother's house,\ndiscontentedly examining old, broken things. The grandmother\nshows her granddaughter where they came from in her house and why they are so dear to her.

A very uncomfortable Crocodile accidentally trashes the store where he works, because he has such a big head and such small paws.… He tries to stay friendly, but gets only condemnation and ridicule in return. An upset Crocodile meets a Raccoon, who also had a bad day. Together they complement each other and solve common problems.
Zhab Zhabych works as an administrator at the reception of the Migratory Birds Hotel. Modest. Punctual. Precise. Polite.

The film tells about the work of Mikhail Afanasyevich Bulgakov in the early 20s of the last century as a journalist in Moscow newspapers and magazines and about his participation in a troupe of traveling actors. In a dream, the writer sees a Gentleman in a gray doublet, whose will Bulgakov cannot resist. But the Gentleman loses to Bulgakov the golden triangle (soul), which brings good luck.
A girl is playing blind man's bluff. As she touches the walls with her eyes closed, scenes from the lives of the house's inhabitants unfold before the viewer.
The story is about a girl and a small sea dragon trapped in a fisherman's net. Based on Chinese myths.
It is believed that the paleo wolf is an ancestor of dogs. 40,000 years ago, a hungry beast and a hunting tribe met on a hunting trail. Following one prey, they had to come together to succeed.\n\n
Animated short film about winning your own place in life
A burning man comes to the main character's birthday and tries to give her a burning gift.\n
This film illustrates an exceptionally friendly relationship.
A film made at Lisa Skvortsova's intensive course "Energy Injection". A story about children's spontaneity.

One night, an unusual creature with the head of a deer comes to a young girl and takes her with him into a magical forest. Who is he? A mysterious magical being or just a figment of her imagination? Will the girl’s younger brother believe he really exists? However, the creature becomes a true friend to the children and opens the gates to a world of wonder.\n

Space traveler Egor crashes on an unknown planet.\nHis ship accidentally blocks the only source of water in the local savannah, and the locals don't like it. To right the wrongs and reconcile the rivals, they will have to work together.
In their last taxi ride, the characters relive the joyful moments of their lives. Their memories intertwine, creating a shared picture of the beautiful and the melancholic.\n

filmEnTitle: The Bride Dog and the Fatal Bells
filmEnPlot: One day, the Dog Bride discovered that she did not have a bouquet. She tried to conjure a bouquet of Claire de lune peonies, but it was not there! Treacherous bluebells filled the valley of the hills. The Bride Dog will have to bring the world back to harmony and balance.

An almanac of pie rhymes made by different directors in different techniques. The yellow diver is a cross–cutting theme and the hero of the almanac. As a yellow submarine, he travels and is surprised by various funny or sad stories, either in the underwater world, or outside your window in the sky, or maybe in a dream before morning tea
An artist girl is uncomfortable at summer camp, but she unexpectedly meets a new friend
A musician playing the piano on a cliff has to fend off a shape-shifting cat that is trying to destroy his music.

A former pearl hunter by fate finds himself the prisoner of a mighty Shipfish, once modified by humans to be a war machine.  The hero explores the creature in an attempt to find a way out, but the fish, embittered at mankind, interferes with him.
An average supermarket worker goes on a wild goose chase in search of himself. An exploration of the topic of simulacra\n
A master from a rickety booth opens other people's locks until love removes the lock from his heart forever.
A girl and her mother bought a live fish at the market…\n

In the old town, Antonov apples fall from the branches, birds not yet flown away chirp, and it seems there are no other sounds in the deserted village. If you decide to click on it, you will receive it. A silence filled with a vast variety of sounds is accessible to her sensitive hearing. But Lucy's neighbor has completely different plans - he came to do some renovations...\n
The two are trying to build a common world, but their own worlds contradict each other.
Sunny has a dream, after which she urgently needs to go on a journey in search of herself and her past.\n
Twelve sketches about my personal and our collective childhood.

Cartoon based on A. Bitov's short story "The Birthmark". A cup of tea, an old magazine and silence — the main character's whole life fits into these simple things. Once again reading a magazine and plunging into a fantasy world, the main character stumbles upon the spread "Wait for me". After that, a dot appears from the turn, which completely changes the hero's world.
A young man returns home during rush hour. The irritation of a noisy and cramped subway car is replaced by an intriguing expectation when a tempting offer from his girlfriend arrives on his phone.
A girl dreams of becoming a bear from the mirror because he can let go of his work.

An animated film about a summer day and children who lost their hats. Bolts, carnations and dry grass are ringing with bells, beetles and power lines are buzzing, a ringing wind is blowing, and an old tin can is sure to hide a treasure inside itself.
A story about a chance meeting, freedom that trunks reach for, and love that sometimes only requires a short stop.\n
Short animated film
The story is about a zoo worker whose main duty is to take care of the internal organs of a giant beast. His usual working day ends with an unusual fluttering of butterflies in his stomach.
Walking through the forest, you can come across more than just trees.

In the silence of the dormitory, the student’s loneliness echoes through the empty corridors. As she enters the shower, unaware eyes follow her – the monster that has been growing closer with each passing day prepares to unveil its true form.
Sometimes, the only way to solve accumulated problems is to get a grip on yourself. Then, help may come unexpectedly, even from otherworldly forces.\n
